reforms.description: The restructuring of the Government's provision of research and development funding led to the establishment of ten Crown Research Institutes (CRIs) in mid-1992, aimed at improving the contribution of science and technology to New Zealand's development.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The establishment of CRIs enhances R&D capabilities, fostering innovation and economic development.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been successfully established and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of CRIs represents a significant structural change in R&D funding and governance.
reforms.source_quote: Ten Crown Research Institutes (CRIs) were established in mid-1992 from the existing government science agencies.

reforms.description: The Government's Achievement Initiative aims to enhance New Zealand's skill base and entrepreneurship through a coherent curriculum structure and a new National Certificate system that allows for competency-based assessment, while also changing immigration policy to attract skilled migrants.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These initiatives are designed to improve educational standards and attract skilled individuals, which will enhance the overall skill level of the workforce.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been successfully implemented and are operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a comprehensive approach to improving human capital in New Zealand.
reforms.source_quote: A key feature of the qualifications reform is a new National Certificate, based on achievement in units of learning which can be acquired at senior level in secondary school or through approved courses.
